title: Crunchy Smoky Semolina Crumble
description: A dairy-free version of a nostalgic Kenyan dish featuring crunchy semolina crumbles with a hint of smokiness.

ingredients.name: 1 cup gluten-free semolina
ingredients.notes: Use certified gluten-free semolina to ensure it's safe for those with gluten sensitivities.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup almond milk or any plant-based milk of choice
ingredients.notes: Almond milk is recommended but feel free to use your preference.

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon coconut oil or vegan butter
ingredients.notes: To prevent sticking, a small amount can be used for greasing the pan if needed.

ingredients.name: Pinch of sea salt
ingredients.notes: To enhance flavors and give a hint of smokiness when toasted.

ingredients.name: 1/2 cup finely chopped walnuts or any other preferred nuts

instructions: In a small mixing bowl, combine semolina, almond milk, coconut oil (or vegan butter), and sea salt. Mix until well combined.
Transfer the mixture into an oven-safe dish or baking tray. Spread it evenly to achieve desired crispness.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until edges are golden brown and crumbly when touched.
In a separate bowl, mix chopped nuts with a pinch of salt (if using) for additional crunchiness.
Allow the semolina mixture to cool slightly before distributing the nuttiness on top. This step will help in making it easier to spread evenly over the semolina crumble.
Return to oven and bake for another 5-7 minutes or until nuts are toasted and lightly golden.
Remove from oven, let cool down, then serve as desired.
